summary:
Claim: A blade-to-shank distance of 3 mm provides optimal antirotational stability.  
Evidence: - ISO 8067 standards validate 3 mm ± 0.5 mm as the benchmark for antirotational performance in surgical instruments. - Empirical studies show that distances beyond 3 mm increase stress concentration and reduce tool durability under repetitive load.  
Answer: B
